food.meaning: A round fruit with thin skin, white flesh, and a core with seeds
food.example: I eat a red apple for a snack because it is sweet, crunchy, and healthy.
food.kid_friendly_explanation: An apple is a fruit that grows on trees. An apple a day keeps the doctor away!
food.sentence: I bit into the juicy apple.

food.meaning: A long curved yellow fruit with soft, creamy flesh inside a peel
food.example: I peel the banana carefully before I eat the soft, sweet inside.
food.kid_friendly_explanation: Bananas are yellow fruits that monkeys love. They give you energy!
food.sentence: The monkey peeled the banana and took a bite.

food.meaning: A round citrus fruit with orange skin and juicy segments inside
food.example: The orange is juicy and tastes sweet and sour. It has lots of vitamin C.
food.kid_friendly_explanation: An orange is a fruit that has vitamin C to keep you from getting sick.
food.sentence: She squeezed the orange to make fresh juice.

food.meaning: A small red fruit with tiny seeds on the outside and a green leafy top
food.example: I put fresh strawberries on my cereal and pancakes for breakfast.
food.kid_friendly_explanation: Strawberries are sweet, red berries that are delicious with whipped cream.
food.sentence: We picked strawberries at the farm.

food.meaning: Small round fruits that grow in bunches on vines
food.example: I eat green grapes for a snack because they are sweet and pop in my mouth.
food.kid_friendly_explanation: Grapes are small, sweet fruits that grow in bunches. You can eat them fresh or dried as raisins.
food.sentence: Mom cut the grapes in half so they were safe to eat.

food.meaning: A large, round fruit with green skin, red juicy flesh, and black seeds
food.example: On hot summer days, I eat cold watermelon to cool down and feel refreshed.
food.kid_friendly_explanation: Watermelon is a big, juicy fruit that is perfect for hot days.
food.sentence: Spit the watermelon seeds as far as you can!

food.meaning: A round baked dish with dough, tomato sauce, cheese, and various toppings
food.example: My favorite pizza has cheese, pepperoni, mushrooms, and olives on top.
food.kid_friendly_explanation: Pizza is a yummy circle of bread with tomato sauce and melted cheese on top.
food.sentence: Let's order a large pepperoni pizza for dinner.

food.meaning: A sandwich with a cooked ground beef patty inside a sliced bun
food.example: My hamburger has lettuce, tomato, onion, pickles, and ketchup on it.
food.kid_friendly_explanation: A hamburger is a sandwich with a meat patty in the middle of a bun.
food.sentence: Would you like a cheeseburger or a hamburger?

food.meaning: A cooked sausage served in a long sliced bun
food.example: I eat a hot dog with mustard and relish at baseball games and barbecues.
food.kid_friendly_explanation: A hot dog is a sausage inside a bun. You can put ketchup or mustard on it.
food.sentence: Put the hot dog in the bun and add your favorite toppings.

food.meaning: Two slices of bread with fillings like meat, cheese, or peanut butter in between
food.example: I eat a peanut butter and jelly sandwich for lunch almost every day.
food.kid_friendly_explanation: A sandwich is bread with yummy things inside like cheese, meat, or peanut butter.
food.sentence: Cut the sandwich into triangles, please.

food.meaning: Long, thin pasta noodles served with sauce, often tomato or meat sauce
food.example: I twirl the spaghetti around my fork and eat it with meatballs and parmesan cheese.
food.kid_friendly_explanation: Spaghetti is long noodles with sauce. You twirl it on your fork.
food.sentence: Don't slurp your spaghetti!

food.meaning: A dish of macaroni pasta mixed with a creamy cheese sauce
food.example: Macaroni and cheese is my favorite comfort food, especially when it's baked with a crunchy top.
food.kid_friendly_explanation: Mac and cheese is noodles with yummy, gooey cheese sauce.
food.sentence: The macaroni and cheese was warm and cheesy.

food.meaning: A liquid food made by boiling vegetables, meat, or fish in water or broth
food.example: The chicken soup is warm and makes me feel better when I have a cold.
food.kid_friendly_explanation: Soup is food in a bowl that you eat with a spoon. It can have noodles or vegetables.
food.sentence: Blow on the hot soup before you eat it.

food.meaning: A mix of raw vegetables, sometimes with fruit, cheese, or meat, served with dressing
food.example: My salad has lettuce, cucumber, tomatoes, carrots, and ranch dressing.
food.kid_friendly_explanation: Salad is a bowl of vegetables. You can add dressing to make it taste good.
food.sentence: Toss the salad with the dressing.

food.meaning: A frozen sweet dessert made from milk, cream, sugar, and flavorings
food.example: I eat chocolate ice cream in a waffle cone on hot summer days.
food.kid_friendly_explanation: Ice cream is a cold, sweet treat. It comes in many flavors like chocolate and vanilla.
food.sentence: My ice cream is melting!

food.meaning: A small, sweet baked treat that can be soft or crunchy
food.example: I dip my warm chocolate chip cookie in a cold glass of milk.
food.kid_friendly_explanation: Cookies are sweet, yummy snacks. Chocolate chip cookies are the most popular.
food.sentence: The cookie was soft and chewy.

food.meaning: A sweet baked dessert often made with flour, sugar, eggs, and frosting
food.example: The chocolate cake has sprinkles, a cherry on top, and a single candle for my birthday.
food.kid_friendly_explanation: Cake is a sweet dessert with frosting. People eat it at birthday parties.
food.sentence: Make a wish and blow out the candles on the cake.

food.meaning: Dried grains or processed food eaten with milk, usually for breakfast
food.example: I eat crunchy cereal with cold milk and sliced bananas on top.
food.kid_friendly_explanation: Cereal is breakfast food in a bowl with milk. Some cereals are sweet, some are healthy.
food.sentence: Pour the cereal into the bowl first, then add the milk.

food.meaning: A flat, round cake made from batter and cooked on a hot surface
food.example: My dad makes fluffy pancakes with maple syrup and butter on Sunday mornings.
food.kid_friendly_explanation: Pancakes are flat, round breakfast cakes. You put syrup on top!
food.sentence: Flip the pancake when you see bubbles.

food.meaning: Food from chickens that can be cooked in many ways like scrambled, fried, or boiled
food.example: I like my eggs scrambled with cheese for breakfast on school days.
food.kid_friendly_explanation: Eggs come from chickens. They are good for breakfast and give you protein.
food.sentence: Crack the egg carefully so the shell doesn't fall in.

food.meaning: A white liquid from cows that is rich in calcium for strong bones
food.example: I drink a glass of cold milk with my cookies for a perfect snack.
food.kid_friendly_explanation: Milk is good for your bones and teeth because it has calcium.
food.sentence: Drink your milk so you grow strong bones.

food.meaning: The liquid extracted from fruits or vegetables
food.example: I drink a small cup of orange juice with my breakfast in the morning.
food.kid_friendly_explanation: Juice is the liquid from fruits like oranges, apples, or grapes.
food.sentence: Would you like apple juice or orange juice?

food.meaning: A food made from flour, water, and yeast, baked in an oven
food.example: I toast my bread and put butter and strawberry jam on it.
food.kid_friendly_explanation: Bread is a soft food made from flour. You use it to make sandwiches or toast.
food.sentence: The bread came out of the oven warm and soft.

food.meaning: A dairy food made from milk that can be soft or hard
food.example: I put a slice of yellow cheese on my hamburger and watch it melt.
food.kid_friendly_explanation: Cheese is made from milk. It comes in many types like cheddar, mozzarella, and Swiss.
food.sentence: The pizza has lots of melted cheese on top.

food.meaning: A thick, creamy dairy food made from fermented milk
food.example: I eat strawberry yogurt with granola for a healthy snack.
food.kid_friendly_explanation: Yogurt is a thick, creamy snack that is good for your tummy.
food.sentence: Peel back the lid and lick the yogurt off it.
